column1: # ZAP Scan Alert Rules
# Format: <alert-id><TAB><action>
# Actions: IGNORE | WARN | FAIL
# See https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/ for the full alert catalogue.
# Default behaviour when no rule matches an alert:
#   riskcode 3 (HIGH)   → FAIL  (blocks CI)
#   riskcode 2 (MEDIUM) → WARN  (reported, does not block)
#   riskcode 1 (LOW)    → WARN
#   riskcode 0 (INFO)   → WARN
# Add an id here only when you have confirmed the finding is a false-positive
# or an accepted risk for this application. Document the reason in the comment.
# ── Deprecated browser controls (no longer enforced by modern browsers) ──────

column3: # X-XSS-Protection Not Set — header is deprecated; CSP replaces it
# Cross-Domain JS Source Inclusion — review manually for unknown 3rd-party origins

column1: # ── CSRF — SPAs typically use SameSite cookies + custom headers instead of tokens
# Demote to WARN; escalate to FAIL if your app has stateful forms without SameSite
# ── Information disclosure — worth investigating, but rarely blocking on its own

column3: # Base64 Disclosure — common FP when non-sensitive encoded data is embedded

column1: # NOTE (2026-07-23): SonarQube Security-by-Design policy (QG-005) treats logging/
# exposure of PII and reserved data as a blocking finding (OWASP A09, CWE-532).
# Kept as WARN here — ZAP's pattern-matching produces frequent false positives —
# but treat any confirmed hit as an accepted-risk candidate requiring sign-off,
# not a silent pass.

column3: # PII Disclosure — review manually; ZAP pattern-matching produces false positives

column1: # ── Content security — review policy complexity before escalating to FAIL
# NOTE (2026-07-23): SonarQube Security-by-Design policy (QG-005) treats missing
# CSP as a blocking "Header di sicurezza HTTP" finding (OWASP A05). Kept as WARN
# here to avoid CI breakage from inline-script false positives in enterprise SPAs
# — escalate to FAIL once the app's CSP policy is finalised.

column3: # CSP Scanner — inline scripts may be intentional in enterprise SPAs; review policy

column1: # ── Mixed content / HTTPS issues — adjust if the scan target is HTTP-only staging

column3: # Viewstate without MAC — relevant only for ASP.NET apps; IGNORE if not applicable

column1: # ── SonarQube Security-by-Design (QG-005) alignment, 2026-07-23 ──────────────
# The categories below are mandatory blocking gates per the Secure Code Analysis
# policy (OWASP Top 10 / ASVS / API Top 10, MITRE CWE Top25, NIST SSDF SP 800-218).
# SonarQube enforces these at the SAST level; the rules here enforce the
# DAST-observable equivalent via ZAP so both scanners agree on what blocks CI.
# Categories with no runtime-observable ZAP signal (hardcoded credentials, weak
# crypto algorithm choice, unvalidated deserialization logic) are SAST-only and
# have no corresponding entry here — SonarQube remains the sole gate for those.
# Injection — SQL / OS command / LDAP (OWASP A03, CWE-89 / CWE-78 / CWE-90)
# XSS and unsanitised output (OWASP A03, CWE-79)
# Insecure deserialization (OWASP A08, CWE-502)

column3: # Java Serialization Object — untrusted deserialized data on the wire

column1: # Path traversal (OWASP A01, CWE-22)
# Insecure protocols — HTTP / weak TLS (OWASP A02, CWE-319 / CWE-326)

column3: # HTTPS Content Available via HTTP — insecure protocol still reachable

column1: # HTTP security headers (OWASP A05)

column3: # Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set

column1: # Error handling — stack trace / technical error exposure (OWASP A05, CWE-209)
